places.name: Grave of Florence Irene Ford
places.description: Florence’s mother built a stairway down to her daughter’s coffin so she could comfort her during storms.

places.name: Under-the-Hill Saloon
places.description: A 200-year-old bar is all that remains of one Mississippi River town's rough-and-tumble past.

places.name: Mammy's Cupboard
places.description: This racially troublesome eatery thought a new coat of paint could erase its offensive connotations.

places.name: 'Turning Angel' Statue
places.description: This monument to the victims of a tragic explosion is said to turn and look at passing cars.

places.name: Longwood
places.description: A grandiose octagonal home crowned by a large dome stands out among the antebellum mansions.

places.name: The Emerald Mound
places.description: The second-largest ceremonial mound in the United States is an artificial hill that is loosely shaped like a pentagon.

places.name: Smoot’s Grocery Blues Lounge
places.description: Catch a live blues set in a former 1930s grocery store on the Mississippi River.

places.name: Under-the-Hill Saloon
places.description: A 200-year-old bar is all that remains of one Mississippi River town's rough-and-tumble past.
